projectDescription: An open-source, participative security engine combining IDS/IPS and WAF with crowdsourced threat intelligence to detect and block malicious IPs across your infrastructure.
keyFeatures: Crowdsourced community blocklist of malicious IPs shared across all participants
Behavior-based detection engine using log analysis and leaky bucket scenarios
Application Security (AppSec/WAF) powered by Coraza for HTTP request inspection
Multi-source log acquisition: files, Docker, Kafka, CloudWatch, Syslog, Loki, S3, and more
Local API (LAPI) and Central API (CAPI) for distributed, multi-agent deployments
Plugin-based notification system supporting Slack, Splunk, email, HTTP, and Sentinel
Hub ecosystem for sharing parsers, scenarios, collections, and AppSec rules under MIT license
Cross-platform support: Linux, Windows, Docker, Kubernetes, and OPNsense
publicApiSurface: POST /v1/watchers/login — authenticate as a CrowdSec agent (machine)
POST /v1/bouncers/login — authenticate as a bouncer (remediation component)
cscli alerts — manage alerts via CLI
cscli decisions — manage bans/decisions via CLI
cscli bouncers — manage remediation components via CLI
cscli machines — manage registered agents via CLI
cscli hub — manage hub content (parsers, scenarios, collections)
cscli appsec-rules / cscli appsec-configs — manage WAF rules
